rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ERNEST V. RHINE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To 1)Quash Production Of Documents At A Deposition 2) Modify Deposition Subpoena 3) Request Protective Order 4) Stay Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable At Authentic Church And 5) Order Monetary Sanctions Against Lydia And Her Attorney Aren Kavcioglu
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Respondent's Motion to Quash Deposition Subpoena to Authentic Church; Modify Subpoena; for Protective Order; to Stay Deposition; for Monetary Sanctions is DENIED. Petitioner's request for sanctions is GRANTED, and sanctions in the amount of $2,400 is awarded to Petitioner. Respondent's request for an order staying the noticed deposition until after Respondent's Motion to Dismiss is heard has been moot for over a month. The court entered the order denying the Motion to Dismiss on March 24, 2021, after hearing. Respondent's request for an order modifying the subpoena language was moot before Respondent filed this motion requesting that relief. On pages three and eight of the motion's Memorandum of Points and Authorities, Respondent states that via meet and confer letters counsel agreed on February 9, 2021 to modify the subpoena's definition of "Temple Baptist Church" to mutually-agreeable language. The parties were able to use the required meet and confer process as intended and resolve this dispute between themselves, without this motion. There is no showing to explain why Respondent was unable or unwilling to use the required meet and confer process to resolve the dispute raised in the motion to quash the deposition subpoena's request for documents numbers 2-7 and 9-10, and for protective order. As shown in Exhibits C and D to the Declaration of Emily Calhoun In Support of the Motion, Petitioner offered to stipulate to a process that would address Respondent's concerns. Specifically, petitioner offered to "stipulate to the entry of a protective order agreeing to limit reasonably the use and/or dissemination of the documents produced by Temple Baptist to this litigation. To the extent there is a particular document produced by Temple Baptist that truly come [sic] within the scope of Evidence Code section 1032 and/or is not reasonably calculated to lead to lead to discovery of admissible evidence, I'm willing to enter into a stipulation that provides for you to initial a meet and confer regarding your objection. If we agree that that document is not discoverable, I will return it and delete all copies in my possession. If we disagree, you may file a motion for a protective order as to any documents you believe should be returned based on a claim of privilege or privacy." (Feb. 9, 2021 letter from Kavcioglu to Calhoun). Respondent did not respond to this meet and confer communication. Instead, Respondent filed the instant motion. Additional discussion between the parties might well have resulted in an agreement that would have made the instant motion unnecessary. The parties previously entered into a five-page Stipulation Re Production of Records From John Braun and Mount Herman, arising from a deposition subpoena Petitioner served on the Person Most Knowledgeable of another charitable religious institution named as a beneficiary in the challenged trust instruments. (Decl. of Armenak Kavcioglu In Support of the Opposition, Exh. 4.) The Stipulation addressed the Gagosian's concerns regarding documents produced by the deponent which were non-discoverable, privileged, or subject to a privacy protection claim. The Stipulation avoided production of privileged materials and allowed production of relevant discovery materials. No motions for protective orders or motions to quash arose from the Mount Herman deposition. [Part 1 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ERNEST V. RHINE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To 1)Quash Production Of Documents At A Deposition 2) Modify Deposition Subpoena 3) Request Protective Order 4) Stay Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable At Authentic Church And 5) Order Monetary Sanctions Against Lydia And Her Attorney Aren Kavcioglu (Added For The Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] There is no showing in the papers as to why Respondent ignored, and thereby rejected, Petitioner's offer to enter into a similar stipulation with respect to the instant deposition. For the first time in the Reply, Respondent asserts that Petitioner's attorney Larry Larson demonstrated an "extreme level of vitriol" as evidenced by the emails attached as Exhibits B, C, and D to her Declaration in Support of the Reply, such that "he cannot be entrusted with even a preview of documentation that might include Kim, Ron, or Ron's mother's private religious or financial information. It is too likely that he would use such sensitive information for cruel leverage and bullying against the Respondents." (Reply at 5:12-19.) The court has reviewed Mr. Larson's four responses contained in the proffered email exchanges. They are dated February 11, 2020, May 7, 2020, and April 8, 2021; they are each between one and five sentences long, and addressed only to counsel. While none of the messages would likely be described as cordial, they do not support Respondent's assertion that Petitioner's counsel would use "sensitive information for cruel leverage and bullying against the Respondents." The court finds that Respondent did not meet and confer in good faith as required by the statute, and did not bring this motion in good faith. Substantively, the motion is incorrect in arguing which party has the initial burden in the context of privilege claims asserted in response to a discovery request. Respondent cites Britt v. Sup. Ct. (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 853, 859, to argue that Petitioner has the initial burden to show that the information sought is directly relevant to the undue influence claim and that disclosure is essential to the resolution of the claim. Not so. The party claiming a privilege in response to a discovery request has the initial burden of proving the preliminary facts to show the privilege applies. (Lopez v. Watchtower Bible & Tract Soc'y of New York, Inc. (2016) 246 CA4th 566, 596, citing Roman Catholic Archbishop of Los Angeles v. Superior Court (2005) 131 Cal.App.4th 417, 442.) The party claiming the privilege generally makes the required preliminary showing by declarations. (Citizens for Ceres v Sup. Ct (2013) 217 CA4th 889, 911.) Neither Respondent nor Ronald Gagosian in his Joinder have met their initial burden of proving the preliminary facts to show the claimed privilege applies. Neither submitted a declaration in support of their claims; the declarations submitted by counsel do not offer support for the privilege claims. As in Watchtower, "Because [the subpoenaed party] had not yet produced a privilege log or identified any specific confidential communications, it had not met its burden to show the preliminary facts supporting the application of the privilege. Thus, its privilege claim was premature." (Id. at 596.) The moving party has not met the initial burden, and the burden has not shifted to Petitioner to show a particularized need for the confidential information sought. (Britt v. Sup.Ct. (San Diego Unified Port Dist.) (1978) 20 C3d 844, 859-862). [Part 2 of 3]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ERNEST V. RHINE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To 1)Quash Production Of Documents At A Deposition 2) Modify Deposition Subpoena 3) Request Protective Order 4) Stay Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable At Authentic Church And 5) Order Monetary Sanctions Against Lydia And Her Attorney Aren Kavcioglu (Added For The Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] Under CCP section 1987.2(a), "the court may in its discretion award the amount of the reasonable expenses incurred in making or opposing the motion, including reasonable attorney's fees, if the court finds the motion was made or opposed in bad faith or without substantial justification or that one or more of the requirements of the subpoena was oppressive." The court finds that this Motion to Quash Deposition Subpoena to Authentic Church; Modify Subpoena; for Protective Order; to Stay Deposition; for Monetary Sanctions was made without substantial justification and without a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented by the motion, which constitutes a misuse of the discovery process. (See CCP sections 2016.040, and 2023.010 sub. (h).) Sanctions are awarded against Respondent and in favor of Petitioner in the amount of $2,400, for the reasonable expenses incurred in opposing this motion. Payment is to be made within 10 days of service of entry of this order. Respondent's request for sanctions presented in her reply papers is denied. This order does not preclude Respondent from objecting to questions posed in the deposition of the Authentic Church (formerly Temple Baptist Church), or any other deposed party in this litigation, on the basis of any relevant evidence code section or asserted privacy interests, or from seeking a protective order based thereon. Petitioner shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling prior to the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on May 5, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 3 of 3]

rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Camille Colarusso Motion To Quash Deposition Subpeona And Objections To Production Of Records
rulings.Rulings: This tentative is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. On February 19, 2021, Counsel for former Conservators Raymond Smith and Jonathan Knott served Deposition Subpoenas for Production of Business Records on Citibank and First Foundation Bank. The subpoenas sought copies of bank records for Thomas Iveli and several business accounts associated with Mr. Iveli. Both Subpoenas requested materials from October 1, 2019 through February 29, 2020. Both subpoenas directed the banks to produce the documents on March 29, 2021 at Pacific Docu-Scan in Concord, California. On March 25, 2021, Objector Camille Collarusso filed a motion to quash both subpoenas. The motion to quash was set for hearing on May 6, 2021. On April 9, 2021, Counsel for Smith and Knott filed an ex parte application for a court order directing Citibank and First Foundation Bank to provide copies of bank statements for the months October 2019 through February 2020 in nine specified accounts. Counsel for Smith and Knott explained in their ex parte application that obtaining these statements was necessary for the former conservators to meet their obligations to the Court in providing bank statements as required by San Francisco Superior Court Local Rule 14.35. On April 26, 2021, Objector Camille Collarusso filed an objection to the ex parte application. On April 27, 2021, the Court granted the ex parte application and ordered Citibank and First Foundation bank to supply copies of the bank statements for the five specified months in the nine specified accounts. On April 30, 2021, Counsel for Smith and Knott informed the Court via email, with a copy to Objector Collarusso, that it was withdrawing the above-described subpoenas to Citibank and First Foundation Bank. Because the subpoenas have been withdrawn, neither bank is required to take any action with respect to producing documents pursuant to the subpoenas. Because both subpoenas issued on February 19, 2021 have been withdrawn, Objector Camille Collarusso's motion to quash them is DENIED as moot. (See Lee v. Swansboro Country Property Owners Assn. (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 575, 580 [when a party agrees to "withdraw its subpoena" it "obviat[es] the need for the trial court to address the merits of plaintiff's motion" to quash.) Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on May 5, 2021. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
